html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 茅亭客话 - The title of the text, meaning 'Tales of the Thatched Pavilion Guest,' refers to a collection of anecdotes or stories.
  • 蜀先兆 - Literally 'the omens of Shu,' referring to the historical context of the Shu region.
  • 歳在甲子 - Refers to the year being the year of the Jiazi cycle in the Chinese zodiac.
  • 兴师伐蜀 - Means 'mobilized troops to attack Shu,' referring to the military campaign against the Shu state.
  • 伪皇太子策勲府 - Refers to the former royal prince's office of merit, now serving as the administrative center.
  • 桃符 - Traditional wooden tablets inscribed with auspicious phrases, often hung on doors during the New Year.
  • 天垂余庆、地接长春 - Auspicious phrases inscribed on the peach wood tablets, symbolizing good fortune and longevity.
  • 太祖皇帝诞圣节号长春 - Refers to the name 'Changchun' given to the emperor's birthday celebration.
  • 先兆皎然 - Means 'clear omens,' indicating that the signs were evident.
  • 国之兴替 - Refers to the rise and fall of a nation.
  • 广政末 - Refers to the end of the Guangzheng era during the Later Shu Dynasty.
  • 破一木 - Means 'cutting open a piece of wood.'
  • 紫纹隶书 - Refers to purple-colored cursive script.
  • 嘉瑞 - Means 'auspicious sign' or 'good omen.'
  • 改太平兴国之号 - Refers to the change of the era name to 'Taiping Xingguo' (Prosperity and Nation-Building).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蜀主 - Refers to the ruler of the Shu state during the Later Shu Dynasty.
  • 吕公余庆 - A historical figure who served as a high-ranking official in the Song Dynasty.
  • 太祖皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Taizu of Song, the founder of the Song Dynasty.
  • 伪太子 - Refers to the former royal prince of the Shu state.
  • 桃符 - Traditional Chinese New Year decorations, often inscribed with auspicious phrases.
  • 天垂余庆、地接长春 - Auspicious phrases that were inscribed on the peach wood tablets.
  • 太祖皇帝诞圣节号长春 - The name given to the emperor's birthday celebration, symbolizing longevity.
  • 国之兴替 - The rise and fall of a nation, often seen as predetermined by fate.
  • 广政末 - The end of the Guangzheng era during the Later Shu Dynasty, marking the decline of the Shu state.
  • 破一木 - Cutting open a piece of wood, which in this context reveals an auspicious sign.
  • 改太平兴国之号 - The change of the era name to 'Taiping Xingguo,' reflecting the new era of peace and prosperity under the Song Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 茅亭客话 - The title of the text.
  • 圣朝干徳二年 - The second year of the Gande era during the Song Dynasty.
  • 兴师伐蜀 - The military campaign against the Shu state.
  • 吕公余庆 - A high-ranking official in the Song Dynasty.
  • 太祖皇帝 - Emperor Taizu of Song.
  • 伪太子 - The former royal prince of the Shu state.
  • 桃符 - Traditional wooden tablets inscribed with auspicious phrases.
  • 天垂余庆、地接长春 - Auspicious phrases inscribed on the peach wood tablets.
  • 太祖皇帝诞圣节号长春 - The name given to the emperor's birthday celebration.
  • 国之兴替 - The rise and fall of a nation.
  • 广政末 - The end of the Guangzheng era during the Later Shu Dynasty.
  • 破一木 - Cutting open a piece of wood.
  • 紫纹隶书 - Purple-colored cursive script.
  • 嘉瑞 - Auspicious sign or good omen.
  • 改太平兴国之号 - The change of the era name to 'Taiping Xingguo.'

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聖宋戊申歳 - Refers to the year 'Wu Shen' in the sexagenary cycle during the reign of the 'Holy Song' dynasty, likely a reference to the Song dynasty.
  • 帝奉元符礼行泰岳 - A formal expression meaning 'the Emperor performed the Yuanfu rites at Mount Tai.'
  • 应天下悉赐大酺 - 'All under heaven were granted da pu,' which refers to a general license for public revelry or celebration, often granted by the emperor.
  • 遵诏旨也 - 'In accordance with the imperial edict,' indicating that the event was officially sanctioned.
  • 未曽见之 - 'Never before seen,' indicating that this was a new or unprecedented event.
  • 荣观尔 - 'A sight of honor,' or 'a glorious spectacle.'
  • 倾动方隅 - 'Shook the surrounding regions,' suggesting the event had a wide-reaching impact.
  • 堕于涂炭 - 'Fallen into the mud and charcoal,' a metaphor for suffering and hardship.
  • 作太平民 - 'To become a people of peace,' or 'to live in peace.'
  • 得观兹盛世矣 - 'To witness this prosperous age,' indicating the people's gratitude and recognition of the current era as a golden age.
  • 甘露降 - 'The sweet dew descended,' a term for ganlu (甘露), a rare and auspicious meteorological event considered a sign of virtue and divine favor.
  • 自承天节日至二十日 - From the Chengtian Jie (承天节) to the 20th day, indicating a specific time period during which the phenomenon occurred.
  • 逐夜联绵不止 - 'Continuously occurring every night without ceasing,' describing the persistence of the phenomenon.
  • 叶无大小，悉皆周徧 - 'Every leaf, whether large or small, was entirely covered,' indicating that the dew was widespread and abundant.
  • 士庶扶老携幼奔驰于路 - 'Scholars and commoners carried the elderly and children and ran along the roads,' showing the widespread public excitement and participation.
  • 以盘盂承接尝 - 'Used bowls and vessels to collect and taste it,' indicating the people's eagerness to partake in the miraculous event.
  • 饮之甘如饴蜜 - 'Drinking it was as sweet as honey,' describing the taste of the ganlu.
  • 里儒证瑞应图曰 - 'The local Confucian scholar who compiled the Ruiying Tu (Book of Auspicious Omens) said,' introducing a source or authority for the explanation of the phenomenon.
  • 王者尊贤尚齿 - 'The ruler honors the wise and respects the elderly,' a Confucian principle that ganlu is said to appear when this virtue is practiced.
  • 道之休明 - 'The Dao is at its most luminous and clear,' indicating a time of moral clarity and virtue.
  • 徳动乾坤而感者，谓之瑞 - 'When virtue moves the heavens and earth and brings about a response, it is called a ruì (auspicious omen),' explaining the nature of ganlu as a sign of virtue.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 聖宋 - Likely a poetic or honorific reference to the Song dynasty, which is often called 'Holy Song' in classical texts.
  • 戊申歳 - The 'Wu Shen' year in the traditional Chinese sexagenary cycle, used to denote a specific year without naming it numerically.
  • 大酺 - A term for a state-sanctioned period of public celebration and revelry, often granted by the emperor to the people.
  • 大圣慈寺 - Great Holy Mercy Temple, likely a major Buddhist temple.
  • 甘露寺 - Sweet Dew Temple, possibly named after the phenomenon described.
  • 净众寺 - Pure Assembly Temple, a Buddhist monastery.
  • 金绳院 - Golden Rope Institute, possibly a Daoist or Buddhist institution.
  • 龙兴观 - Dragon Ascendance Temple, a Daoist temple.
  • 青羊宫 - Green Sheep Palace, a Daoist temple.
  • 衙廨内道院 - The Daoist hall within the government office.
  • 承天节 - A specific festival or date, possibly a celebration of the emperor or a significant imperial event.
  • 里儒证瑞应图 - A local Confucian scholar who compiled the Ruiying Tu (Book of Auspicious Omens), a classical text that recorded signs and omens believed to indicate the virtue of the ruler or the will of heaven.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大中祥符六年 - Specific year in the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of Song (1013 AD).
  • 栢柱上有木纹如画天尊状 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'a wooden pillar with wood grain resembling a painting of the Heavenly Lord.'
  • 毛髪眉目衣服履舄纤缕悉备 - Detailed description of the carved figure, meaning 'hair, eyebrows, eyes, clothing, shoes, and every detail are fully depicted.'
  • 知州比部佐郎刘公宗言遂绘事奏闻 - Formal phrase meaning 'Governor Liu Gong Zongyan then painted the image and reported it to the court.'
  • 奉圣旨令津置赴阙 - Formal phrase meaning 'the imperial decree ordered it to be transported to the capital.'
  • 送玉清昭应宫 - Formal phrase meaning 'sent to the Jade Clear Palace of Response.'
  • 今川民皆图画供养之 - Formal phrase meaning 'now the people of Sichuan all paint and worship it.'
  • 虎盗屏迹 - Formal phrase meaning 'tigers and bandits disappeared.'
  • 剑利之间虎暴尤甚 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the tigers were especially fierce between Jian and Li.'
  • 白卫岭、石筒磎 - Place names: 'White Guard Mountain' and 'Stone Tube Ravine.'
  • 虎名披鬃子 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the tiger was named 'Fur Coated.'
  • 地号税人场 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the place was called 'Tax Man Field.'
  • 绵汉间白杨林 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'between Mian and Han, the White Poplar Forest.'
  • 虎名裂蹄子 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the tiger was named 'Split Hoof.'
  • 商旅聚徒而行 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'merchants and travelers gathered in groups to travel.'
  • 屡有遭搏噬者 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'many were attacked and devoured.'
  • 嘉州牛颈山有子母虎 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'in Jiuzhou, there were mother and cub tigers on Cow Neck Mountain.'
  • 陵州铁炉山有青豹子 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'in Lingzhou, there was a green leopard on Iron Furnace Mountain.'
  • 彭蜀近山镇县暴兽成羣 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'in the nearby mountains of Peng and Shu, fierce animals formed packs.'
  • 农家不敢放牧 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'farmers dared not let their livestock graze.'
  • 出门采樵行旅共苦之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'those who went out to gather firewood and travelers all suffered from it.'
  • 羣盗诸州县 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'bandits in various states and counties.'
  • 结聚各有百人至二百人 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'gathered in groups of one hundred to two hundred people.'
  • 官军掩捕则与格斗 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'when government troops tried to capture them, they fought back.'
  • 胜则御敌官军 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'if they won, they would attack the government troops.'
  • 败则奔入林薮 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'if they lost, they would flee into the forests.'
  • 虽有捕盗之吏，莫能擒获 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'even though there were officials tasked with capturing bandits, none could catch them.'
  • 仅四十余年 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'for nearly forty years.'
  • 民无安业 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the people had no stable livelihood.'
  • 圣朝克复后 - Formal phrase meaning 'after the Holy Court regained control.'
  • 庐舍骈接 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'houses were built side by side.'
  • 犬豕纵横 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'dogs and pigs roamed freely.'
  • 虎豹羣盗悉皆屏迹 - Formal phrase meaning 'tigers, leopards, and bandits all disappeared.'
  • 得非系国朝之盛衰，时政之能否乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is this not due to the prosperity or decline of the state and the effectiveness of its policies?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天尊 - A title for a high-ranking deity or sage, often used in religious contexts.
  • 大中祥符六年 - Specific year in the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of Song (1013 AD).
  • 绵州彰明县崇仙观 - Specific location: Changming County in Mianzhou, home to the Chongxian Temple.
  • 玉清昭应宫 - Specific temple: the Jade Clear Palace of Response, a significant religious site.
  • 赐紫 - Formal bestowal of purple robes, a sign of high rank or honor.
  • 茶绢等物 - Tea and silk, which were valuable commodities and symbols of status.
  • 剑利之间 - Specific region: between Jian and Li, likely referring to areas in Sichuan.
  • 白卫岭、石筒磎 - Specific locations: White Guard Mountain and Stone Tube Ravine.
  • 绵汉间 - Specific region: between Mian and Han, likely referring to areas in Sichuan.
  • 嘉州牛颈山 - Specific location: Cow Neck Mountain in Jiuzhou.
  • 陵州铁炉山 - Specific location: Iron Furnace Mountain in Lingzhou.
  • 彭蜀近山镇县 - Specific region: the nearby mountains of Peng and Shu, likely referring to areas in Sichuan.
  • 圣朝克复后 - Refers to the period after the Song Dynasty regained control over certain regions, possibly after the rebellion or invasion of the Western Xia.
  • 庐舍骈接 - Describes the dense population and development of the area.
  • 犬豕纵横 - Describes the chaotic and wild state of the area before the restoration.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蜀无大水 - Literal phrase meaning 'Shu has no great flood,' setting the tone for the narrative.
  • 开宝五年壬申歳秋八月初 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar, indicating the time period of the event.
  • 岷江暴涨 - Describes the sudden increase in the water level of the Min River.
  • 永康军大堰将坏 - Indicates the imminent collapse of a large dam in Yongkang Military District.
  • 水入府江 - Describes the water entering the capital river.
  • 知军薛舍人文宝与百姓忧惶 - Describes the anxiety of the military governor Xue She and the people.
  • 但见惊波怒涛声如雷吼 - Vivid description of the turbulent waves and roaring sounds.
  • 中流有一巨材 - Describes a large object floating in the middle of the river.
  • 随骇浪而下 - Indicates the object moving downstream with the waves.
  • 近而观之，乃一大蛇耳 - Reveals that the object is actually a large snake.
  • 举头横身截于堰上 - Describes the snake's position on the dam.
  • 至其夜闻堰上呼噪之声 - Describes the noise heard on the dam at night.
  • 列炬纵横 - Describes the arrangement of torches.
  • 虽大风暴雨，火影不灭 - Indicates that despite the strong wind and rain, the fire did not extinguish.
  • 平旦，广济王李公祠内旗帜皆濡湿 - Describes the state of the flags in the Guangji Temple at dawn.
  • 堰上唯见一面，沙堤堰水入新津江口 - Describes the situation on the dam and the flow of water into the Xinzhen River.
  • 嘉眉州漂溺至甚，而府江不溢 - Contrasts the severe flooding in Jiamian Prefecture with the lack of overflow in the capital river.
  • 李冰自秦时代张若为蜀守 - Introduces Li Bing, who served as the governor of Shu during the Qin Dynasty.
  • 实有道之士也 - Indicates that Li Bing was a person of virtue.
  • 蜀困水难，至于臼灶生蛙，人罹垫溺且久矣 - Describes the long-standing water problems in Shu.
  • 公以道法役使鬼神擒捕水怪 - Describes Li Bing's use of Daoist methods to control water spirits.
  • 因是壅止泛浪，凿山离堆，辟沫水于南北为二江 - Describes Li Bing's actions to control the water and create two rivers.
  • 灌溉彭汉 - Refers to the irrigation of the land in the three prefectures of Shu.
  • 三石人 - Refers to the stone figures Li Bing created to control water levels.
  • 石犀 - Refers to the stone rhinoceroses Li Bing created to subdue water spirits.
  • 陆海 - Refers to a fertile land.
  • 水潦之虞 - Refers to the fear of water disasters.
  • 万井富实 - Describes the prosperity and wealth of the region.
  • 禹 - Refers to Yu the Great, a legendary figure known for his water management skills.
  • 江浒 - Refers to the banks of the river.
  • 祠宇 - Refers to temples or shrines.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开宝五年壬申歳秋八月初 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar, indicating the time period of the event.
  • 永康军 - Refers to a military district in the Sichuan region.
  • 广济王李公祠 - Refers to a temple dedicated to Li Bing, a historical figure known for his water management projects.
  • 李冰 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Shu during the Qin Dynasty and is famous for his water management projects.
  • 张若 - Another historical figure who served as the governor of Shu during the Qin Dynasty.
  • 臼灶生蛙 - A metaphor for the severe water problems in Shu, where frogs were seen in kitchen stoves.
  • 人罹垫溺 - Refers to the suffering of people due to water disasters.
  • 道法 - Refers to Daoist methods and practices.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to spirits and deities in Chinese mythology.
  • 水怪 - Refers to water spirits or monsters.
  • 壅止泛浪 - Describes the act of stopping the waves.
  • 凿山离堆 - Describes the act of carving mountains to create barriers.
  • 辟沫水于南北为二江 - Describes the creation of two rivers to manage water flow.
  • 灌溉彭汉 - Refers to the irrigation of the land in the three prefectures of Shu.
  • 三石人 - Refers to the stone figures Li Bing created to control water levels.
  • 石犀 - Refers to the stone rhinoceroses Li Bing created to subdue water spirits.
  • 陆海 - Refers to a fertile land.
  • 水潦之虞 - Refers to the fear of water disasters.
  • 万井富实 - Describes the prosperity and wealth of the region.
  • 禹 - Refers to Yu the Great, a legendary figure known for his water management skills.
  • 江浒 - Refers to the banks of the river.
  • 祠宇 - Refers to temples or shrines.

Proper Nouns

  • 永康军 - Military district in the Sichuan region.
  • 广济王李公祠 - Temple dedicated to Li Bing, a historical figure known for his water management projects.
  • 李冰 - Historical figure who served as the governor of Shu during the Qin Dynasty and is famous for his water management projects.
  • 张若 - Historical figure who served as the governor of Shu during the Qin Dynasty.
  • 禹 - Legendary figure known for his water management skills.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 車轍跡 - Tracks of a carriage, used metaphorically to suggest a divine or supernatural event.
  • 彩霧輕煙 - Describes a scene of colorful mist and light smoke, evoking a mystical or ethereal atmosphere.
  • 月光如晝 - Moonlight as bright as day, emphasizing the intensity and clarity of the moonlight.
  • 香風瑞氣 - Fragrant winds and auspicious vapors, associated with divine or supernatural phenomena.
  • 四逺村民登層巒而望之 - Villagers from far and wide climbing high hills to observe the scene, indicating a widespread and significant event.
  • 遲明 - By dawn or in the early morning, indicating the passage of time.
  • 碾土深三四寸 - Soil crushed to a depth of three or four inches, suggesting the significant impact of the event.
  • 其轍跡隨山勢高下 - Tracks following the contours of the mountain, indicating the natural and divine nature of the event.
  • 迤逦狹小 - Gradually narrowing, describing the tracks becoming smaller as they approach the cave.
  • 音樂之聲昼夜不絕 - Sound of music never ceases day or night, emphasizing the continuous and supernatural nature of the event.
  • 遂聞諸州縣 - Thus it was heard throughout the various states and counties, indicating the widespread impact of the event.
  • 殿前承旨 - High-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 兵马监押 - Military officer responsible for overseeing troops.
  • 知县事 - County magistrate.
  • 縣尉 - County sheriff or constable.
  • 尋詣仙洞 - They went to the fairy cave, indicating a visit to a sacred or supernatural place.
  • 觀茲轍跡樂聲 - To observe these tracks and the music, emphasizing the purpose of their visit.
  • 以事奏聞 - To report the matter to the emperor, indicating the official nature of their actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 羅璝山 - Luoxiu Mountain, a specific location in Luoyang.
  • 羅璝洞 - Luoxiu Cave, a sacred place associated with the legendary figure Luo Qiu.
  • 羅真人名璝 - Luo Qiu, the legendary figure associated with the cave.
  • 修道上升之所也 - Place where Luo Qiu practiced Taoism and ascended to immortality.
  • 其洞凡有水旱疾癘 - Belief that the cave has the power to influence natural phenomena such as floods, droughts, and diseases.
  • 太平興國五年庚辰歳中秋 - Mid-Autumn Festival of the year 980, during the reign of Emperor Taizong of the Song Dynasty.
  • 殿前承旨兵马监押知县事陈覃 - Chen Tan, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 縣尉邹崇让 - Zou Chongrang, the county sheriff or constable.

Proper Nouns

  • 羅璝山 - Luoxiu Mountain.
  • 羅璝洞 - Luoxiu Cave.
  • 羅真人名璝 - Luo Qiu, the legendary figure associated with the cave.
  • 太平興國五年庚辰歳中秋 - Mid-Autumn Festival of the year 980.
  • 殿前承旨兵马监押知县事陈覃 - Chen Tan, a high-ranking official.
  • 縣尉邹崇让 - Zou Chongrang, the county sheriff or constable.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诏大九井山虎耳先生李洞宾赍香于洞前，设醮礼察视之，由以祈灵贶 - Key phrases describing the ritual and spiritual significance: '赍香于洞前' (carry incense to the cave entrance) and '设醮礼察视之' (set up a ritual and inspect the cave).
  • 先生步无差跌，神气自若，出洞之时，衣履之上无泥滓沾污之迹 - Describes Li Dongbin's calm demeanor and the pristine condition of his clothes and shoes after exiting the cave.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大九井山 - A specific mountain in China, known for its natural beauty and spiritual significance.
  • 虎耳先生李洞宾 - A respected figure in Taoist lore, often associated with longevity and wisdom.
  • 李八百 - A nickname given to Li Dongbin, suggesting he is 800 years old, though he appears to be around 50.
  • 设醮礼 - A traditional Taoist ritual involving the burning of incense and offerings to deities.
  • 魂竦汗沥 - Describes a state of intense fear or awe, often associated with supernatural experiences.

Proper Nouns

  • 大九井山 - A specific mountain.
  • 虎耳先生李洞宾 - A respected Taoist figure.
  • 李八百 - A nickname for Li Dongbin.
  • 设醮礼 - A specific Taoist ritual.
  • 灵贶 - Divine favor.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 垦耕力作 - Tilling and farming with great effort, describing the hard work of the villagers.
  • 人质鄙朴而性慈仁 - Plain and simple in nature but kind and benevolent, describing Mr. Junyou's character.
  • 行见禽兽，常下道回避，不欲惊之 - When he saw animals, he would always step aside to avoid them, not wanting to disturb them, showing his gentle nature.
  • 寡讷少与人交言 - He was shy and spoke little with others, indicating his reserved personality.
  • 年六十许，凡见山人道士聚，得佣负之直以接奉之 - At the age of sixty, whenever he saw hermits or Taoist priests gathering, he would hire himself out to carry their belongings and receive payment to serve them, highlighting his respect for spiritual figures.
  • 或遗其钱即不顾而回 - If they left him money, he would not look back, indicating his humility and detachment from material gain.
  • 如此率以为常 - He did this regularly, emphasizing his consistent behavior.
  • 开宝九年春 - The year 976 AD, during the Song Dynasty.
  • 往云顶山寺遇一道士 - He went to Yunding Mountain Temple and met a Taoist priest, setting the scene for the encounter.
  • 古貌神俊 - Having an ancient appearance and divine grace, describing the Taoist priest.
  • 布衣麄帻 - Wearing a simple robe and a coarse headband, indicating his humble attire.
  • 引一黑狗 - Leading a black dog, adding a detail to the description.
  • 愿与我携拄杖药囊到青城山，当倍酬尔直 - I wish you would carry my walking stick and medicine bag to Qingcheng Mountain, and I will pay you double for your trouble, showing the priest's request and promise of reward.
  • 君友忻然随之 - Mr. Junyou gladly followed him, indicating his willingness to help.
  • 入一小径 - They entered a small path, setting the scene for their journey.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 遂州小溪县石城镇仙女垭村 - A specific village in the Sichuan region, known for its natural beauty and historical significance.
  • 开宝九年 - The year 976 AD, during the reign of Emperor Taizu of Song, a period of relative peace and cultural flourishing.
  • 云顶山寺 - A temple located on Yunding Mountain, known for its spiritual significance and natural beauty.
  • 青城山 - A famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan, known for its scenic beauty and spiritual importance.
  • 山人道士 - Refers to hermits and Taoist priests, who were often respected for their wisdom and spiritual practices.

  • 休复尝读登真隐诀，谓仙道有升天蹑云者，游行五岳者，服饵不死者，尸解而仙者 - Refers to a text or tradition: 'I have also read the *Dengzhen Yinde* (Secrets of Ascending to the True), which speaks of the ways of immortality: those who ascend to heaven and tread clouds, those who travel among the Five Great Mountains, those who eat elixirs to achieve immortality, and those who undergo '尸解' to become immortals.'
  • 忽有蹔游太阴，自有太一守尸，三魂营骨，七魄卫肉，胎灵録气虽以铁石牢固藏闭，终至炼形数满，当自擘石飞空而仙者 - Describes a mystical process: 'Suddenly, there is a brief journey to the dark region (太阴), where the Great One (太一) guards the body, the three souls nurture the bones, and the seven spirits protect the flesh. Even if the primordial spirit is locked away in iron and stone, eventually, after repeated transformations, the body will break through the rock and fly into the void to become an immortal.'
  • 夫得道之士，入火不烁，入水不濡，蹑空如履实，触实如蹈虚 - Describes the abilities of those who have attained the Dao: 'Those who have attained the Dao can enter fire without being scorched, enter water without being wet, walk on empty space as if on solid ground, and touch solid ground as if it were empty.'
  • 虽九地之厚、巨海之广、八极之逺、万方之大，应倐欻而至，何所拘滞耶 - Means 'Even the depth of the nine regions, the breadth of the great seas, the distance of the eight corners of the universe, and the vastness of all directions, can be reached instantly, so why should they be bound or obstructed?'
  • 所以然者，形与道合，道无不在，毫芒之细、万物之众，道皆有之 - Explains the reason: 'This is because the form is united with the Dao, which is everywhere. The Dao exists in the finest details and in all things.'
  • 今备録者，与王客张本也 - Means 'Now, this is recorded in full, as a basis for the teachings of Master Zhang.'

  • 童子具以崔射覆为对 - The child fully answered with Cui's response to the divination. '具以' means 'fully with,' and '射覆' is a form of divination where one guesses the object hidden under a covering.
  • 洞卿久知崔有道 - Dong Qing had long known that Cui had the Dao. '有道' means 'had the Dao' or 'was virtuous.'
  • 令童子握空拳再指此字 - He ordered the child to hold an empty fist and point to this character. '再指' means 'point to again' or 'point to once more.'
  • 崔曰：空拳尔 - Cui said: It's just an empty fist. '尔' is a particle that can mean 'just' or 'only.'
  • 洞卿亲诣崔 - Dong Qing personally went to see Cui. '亲诣' means 'personally went to see.'
  • 一字而射覆者三，皆不同 - Three times, the character was divined and each time it was different. '一字' means 'one character,' and '射覆者三' means 'three times the divination.'
  • 非有道讵能及此 - Only someone with the Dao could achieve this. '讵能' means 'could possibly' or 'could achieve.'
  • 崔曰：皆是童子先言，非老夫能知尔 - Cui said: It was all the child who spoke first; I, the old man, don't know it. '皆是' means 'all is,' and '非老夫能知尔' means 'not the old man's ability to know.'
  • 此字象萝卜，亦象梳，亦象空拳 - This character resembles a radish, also a comb, also an empty fist. '象' means 'resembles' or 'is like.'
  • 伯盛之后，展拓子城 - After Bo Sheng, the inner city was expanded. '展拓' means 'expanded' or 'extended.'
  • 辉焕通衢，署曰得贤楼 - They shone brightly over the main streets, and were named the Virtuous Talent Pavilion. '辉焕' means 'shone brightly,' and '署曰' means 'named as.'
  • 玉局化尊像并迁就龙兴观 - The Jade Bureau's revered images were moved to Longxing Temple. '并迁' means 'were moved together.'

  • 时杜天师诣崔 - At that time, Master Du visited Cui. '诣' means 'visited' or 'went to see.'
  • 曰：今主上迁移仙化，其有证应乎 - He said: Is there any evidence that the current ruler has been moved by divine transformation? '迁移仙化' means 'moved by divine transformation,' and '其有证应乎' means 'is there any evidence?'
  • 崔叹息良久，言曰：皇嗣作难尔 - Cui sighed for a long time, and said: It is because the heir apparent will cause trouble. '良久' means 'for a long time,' and '皇嗣作难尔' means 'it is because the heir apparent will cause trouble.'

  • 寻伏诛 - Means that he was soon executed, '寻' indicating a short time after the event.

  • 非王氏不可也 - Suggests that only the Wang family can restore the situation, emphasizing the inevitability of their involvement.
  • 先主殂 - Refers to the death of the first ruler, '先主' meaning the former emperor.

  • 知州谏大夫凌公策奏乞移王先主祠 - Indicates that Governor and Counselor-in-Adviser Ling Gong Cai proposed moving the shrine of the first Wang ruler, '知州' meaning governor, '谏大夫' meaning counselor-in-adviser, and '奏乞' meaning to submit a request.
  • 取其材植以修此化 - Means to use the materials from the shrine to repair or renovate the transformation, '材植' referring to the materials and resources.

  • 观者千余人 - Indicates that over a thousand people watched the event, '观者' meaning spectators.

  • 寻画图呈进 - Means that a drawing of the event was soon presented, '寻' indicating a short time after the event, and '画图' meaning a drawing or illustration.
  • 降诏奬谕 - Indicates that an imperial edict was issued praising the event, '降诏' meaning to issue an imperial decree, and '奬谕' meaning to commend and praise.
  • 何其逺哉 - Suggests that the event is far-reaching or significant, '何其' meaning how, and '逺' meaning far or distant.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其传忽先经以始事，忽后经以终义 - Describes the erratic use of the *Zuo Zhuan* in relation to the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 忽依经以辩理，忽错经以合异 - Highlights the inconsistent method of referencing the original text.
  • 广记备言以成一家之通体尔 - Explains the purpose of the *Zuo Zhuan* as a comprehensive collection.
  • 不思孔子修经与诗书周易为等 - Critiques the lack of recognition of the *Zuo Zhuan*'s status.
  • 列丘明之传当与司马迁班固为等列 - Argues for the *Zuo Zhuan*'s equal status with other historical works.
  • 岂合将经之年与传之年相附参而贯之 - Questions the appropriateness of combining dates from different sources.
  • 将令学者素无资禀纵意自裁 - Critiques the lack of proper training among scholars.
  • 但务声律，罔知古道 - Critiques the superficial focus on phonetics and rhythm.
  • 将周孔之圣贤班马之文章，皆不由兹制作，靡得而达焉 - Critiques the idea that the *Zuo Zhuan* is essential for understanding classical works.
  • 然皇王帝霸之道，兴亡理乱之体，其可闻乎 - Questions the validity of the approach.
  • 遂引证当时以左传文为春秋者数人 - Mentions contemporary scholars who cite the *Zuo Zhuan* as the source of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伪蜀举人张洸 - Pseudo-Shu juren Zhang Guang – '伪蜀' refers to the short-lived state of Former Shu, a kingdom established by Meng Zhixiang in the late 9th century. '举人' is a degree in the imperial examination system, signifying a candidate who has passed the county-level examination.
  • 雍熙丙戌歳 - In the year Bing Xu of the Yongxi era – '雍熙' is the reign era of Emperor Taizong of Song, and '丙戌' is a stem-branch year designation (corresponding to 986 CE).
  • 往嘉州谒平羌令 - went to Jia Prefecture to visit the magistrate of Pingqiang – '谒' means to visit or pay respects to an official.
  • 船次平羌溉下夜泊 - the boat anchored at night below Pingqiang Gai – '次' means to stop or moor, and '溉下' may be a specific location near Pingqiang.
  • 容貌端俨 - appearance dignified and solemn – a classical way of describing a respectful or solemn demeanor.
  • 白衣华焕 - white robes radiant and resplendent – a metaphor for purity or perhaps a reference to a ghost or spirit.
  • 俯伏求救 - knelt and begged for help – a formal gesture of supplication.
  • 跳踯之声不已 - the sound of jumping and leaping did not cease – describes the noise made by the fish.
  • 性躁急不能容物 - temperament was hasty and impatient, unable to tolerate others – a description of Zhang Guang's personality.
  • 怒此鱼挠其寝 - angry that this fish disturbed his sleep – '挠' means to disturb or annoy.
  • 扶栈取鱼弃于江中 - he climbed onto the boat's platform, took the fish, and threw it into the river – '扶栈' refers to climbing onto the boat's structure.
  • 复梦二白衣持大蒜数头恳谢而去 - again dreamed of the two white-robed figures holding several heads of garlic and earnestly thanking him before departing – '数头' literally means 'several heads' but in context refers to a few cloves of garlic.
  • 迟明方悟向梦者鱼也 - It was only at dawn that he realized the dreamer was the fish – '迟明' means 'dawn' or 'late morning.'
  • 延君筭尔 - you will be extended the calculation of your lifespan – a cryptic or metaphorical statement. '筭' can mean 'calculation' or 'fate,' and this phrase may be a pun or riddle.
  • 后隐书三卷 - three volumes of *Hou Yin Shu* – a title of a work written by Zhang Guang in his later years, possibly a philosophical or mystical text.
  • 享寿七十八而卒 - lived to be seventy-eight and died – a standard expression for recording a person’s lifespan and death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伪蜀 - Pseudo-Shu refers to the Former Shu state, a short-lived kingdom established in the late 9th century by Meng Zhixiang, which was later conquered by the Later Liang. It is called '伪' (false) by later dynasties, such as the Song, to denote its illegitimacy.
  • 举人 - A degree in the imperial examination system, indicating that Zhang Guang had passed the county-level exam but had not yet taken the higher-level exams (e.g., the metropolitan or palace examinations).
  • 雍熙 - The reign title of Emperor Taizong of the Song Dynasty (r. 976–997), during which the Song Dynasty expanded its influence and consolidated its power in southern China.
  • 嘉州 - The ancient name for modern-day Leshan City in Sichuan Province.
  • 平羌 - A district or town in Sichuan, likely near the river.
  • 平羌溉下 - A specific location near Pingqiang, possibly a riverbank or landing point.
  • 梦鱼 - Dreaming of fish is a common motif in Chinese literature and folklore, often symbolizing transformation, fate, or supernatural encounters.
  • 白衣 - White-clad can refer to ghosts, spirits, or people in mourning, but in this context, it is used to describe the two figures in Zhang Guang's dream.
  • 大蒜 - Garlic is a common food item in Chinese culture but also appears in folklore and religious contexts, sometimes associated with warding off evil or healing.
  • 延君筭尔 - This phrase is likely a pun or metaphor. '筭' can mean 'calculation' or 'fate,' and the phrase may be interpreted as 'you will be extended your lifespan' or 'your fate will be extended.'
  • 后隐书 - A title of a book written by Zhang Guang in his later years. The term '隐书' may imply a hidden or esoteric work, possibly related to Daoist or mystical philosophy.

Proper Nouns

  • 张洸 - Zhang Guang – a proper name, the subject of the passage.
  • 字海上 - styled Hai Shang – a common practice in classical Chinese to give a person a courtesy name.
  • 伪蜀 - Pseudo-Shu – a historical reference to the Former Shu state.
  • 雍熙 - Yongxi – the reign era of Emperor Taizong of the Song Dynasty.
  • 丙戌 - Bing Xu – a stem-branch year designation.
  • 嘉州 - Jia Prefecture – the ancient name for modern-day Leshan, Sichuan.
  • 平羌 - Pingqiang – a district or town in Sichuan.
  • 平羌溉下 - Pingqiang Gai – a specific location near Pingqiang.
  • 后隐书 - Hou Yin Shu – the title of the book written by Zhang Guang in his later years.
  • 七十八 - seventy-eight – the age at which Zhang Guang died.

  • 檀越供须都不谢 - Means that the master does not thank donors for their offerings, '檀越' referring to benefactors or donors.
  • 沿身不直五分铜 - Implies that the master's worth is not measured in material terms, '沿身' meaning the entire person, and '五分铜' a small amount of copper money.
  • 一句玄玄岂论价 - Suggests that the master's wisdom is priceless and cannot be quantified, '玄玄' meaning profound or mysterious.
  • 貌古神清不可画 - Describes the master's appearance as ancient and spirit as clear, beyond the ability to be captured in a painting, '不可画' meaning cannot be painted.
  • 鹤性云情本自然 - Implies that the master's nature is as free and natural as a crane and clouds, '鹤性' referring to the nature of a crane, and '云情' the mood of clouds.
  • 生死无心全不怕 - Suggests that the master is completely fearless of life and death, '无心' meaning without attachment or fear.
  • 总逢刼火未为灾 - Implies that even in the face of great calamities, the master remains unharmed, '刼火' referring to the fires of the end times.
  • 暗里龙神应叹讶 - Suggests that even the hidden spirits and dragons are amazed by the master's state, '暗里' meaning secretly or hidden, and '叹讶' meaning to marvel or be astonished.
  • 不说禅，不答话 - Indicates that the master does not speak about Zen or engage in conversation, '不说禅' meaning does not talk about Zen, and '不答话' meaning does not answer questions.
  • 葢为人心难诱化 - Suggests that the master avoids speaking because it is difficult to guide or transform people, '难诱化' meaning hard to guide or transform.
  • 尽奔名利漫驱驱 - Describes people rushing after fame and profit, '漫驱驱' implying aimlessly and hurriedly.
  • 个个何曾有般若 - Suggests that most people lack true wisdom, '般若' meaning wisdom or insight.
  • 分明与说速休心 - Implies that the master clearly tells people to stop their minds from wandering, '速休心' meaning to quickly calm the mind.
  • 供家却道也烂也 - Suggests that despite the master's efforts, some people still dismiss the teachings as trivial, '也烂也' meaning 'it's just trash.'

  • 优礼待之 - 'Treated him with honor and respect.'
  • 禅客须知集 - A compilation for Zen practitioners, likely containing practical advice or guidelines.
  • 禅宗祖裔图 - A genealogy or lineage chart of the Zen Buddhist tradition.
  • 阐道歌行偈颂 - A collection of songs, verses, and hymns that expound the Dharma.
  • 题曰禅宗至道集 - 'Titled *The Ultimate Dharma of the Zen School*.'
  • 行于世 - 'Circulated in the world' or 'became known in the world.'
  • 张平云 - A name, likely a lay Buddhist practitioner.
  • 学释氏法 - 'Studied the teachings of the Buddha' or 'practiced the Dharma of the Buddha.'
  • 人谓之居士 - 'People called him a *layman*' or '*householder*', a term for lay Buddhist practitioners.
  • 不拘生死者 - 'One who is not bound by birth and death,' a reference to enlightenment or liberation.
  • 愿师直指 - 'May the teacher point it out directly,' a common phrase in Zen asking for direct transmission.
  • 非干日月照，昼夜自分明 - A Zen saying meaning 'Not relying on the sun and moon to illuminate, day and night it is clear on its own,' suggesting inner illumination or self-realization.
  • 百亿往来非指的光明终不碍山河时 - A complex Zen expression. 'When the light of the non-pointed (i.e., the ineffable) does not hinder the mountains and rivers, even in the hundredfold coming and going,' suggesting the transcendence of dharma beyond form.
  • 红尾漫揺三尺浪 - A metaphorical image, possibly meaning 'the red tail sways in three-foot waves,' a poetic image used to describe the dynamic nature of reality or the mind.
  • 真龙透石本无踪 - 'The true dragon passes through stone, leaving no trace,' a metaphor for the ineffable nature of enlightenment or the Buddha-nature.
  • 叅玄録 - A record of Zen meditation or inquiry into the profound.
  • 玄珠集 - A collection of 'mysterious pearls,' likely a compilation of Zen insights or teachings.
  • 歌行句偈百余篇 - 'Over a hundred poems, verses, and songs.'
  • 毳流来问我家风 - 'A traveler in coarse robes comes to ask about my family tradition,' a metaphor for a disciple or visitor seeking to understand the master's teachings.
  • 我道玲珑处处通 - 'I say that everywhere is clear and open,' suggesting the universality of the Dharma.
  • 顷刻万邦皆徧到 - 'In an instant, all nations are reached,' a metaphor for the boundless reach of the Dharma or the mind.
  • 途中曽未见人逢 - 'On the way, I have never met anyone,' suggesting the solitude or isolation of the spiritual path.
  • 仙化三日 - 'Transcended in three days,' referring to the death or passing of a sage.
  • 口吐气满屋氛氲 - 'Spewed breath filling the room with fragrance,' a description of the spiritual or physical signs of a master's passing.
  • 弟子告云 - 'The disciple said,' introducing a dialogue or report.
  • 宗门秪以眼目为先 - 'The Zen gate (school) only takes the eyes as the priority,' suggesting that vision or perception is the key to realization.
  • 不以睡相为事 - 'Does not take sleeping posture as a matter,' implying that the Zen school does not emphasize physical postures or rituals.
  • 言讫，香气乃絶 - 'After speaking, the fragrance ceased,' marking the end of the master's presence or the completion of the spiritual transmission.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大觉禅师 - A title for a prominent Zen master; the name '慈觉' (Cici) and '法天' (Fati) are the monk's given name and courtesy name.
  • 王蜀末 - Refers to the end of the Western Shu Kingdom, one of the Three Kingdoms.
  • 孟蜀初 - Refers to the early period of the Later Shu Kingdom, a kingdom that emerged after the fall of Western Shu during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 莲经 - The *Lotus Sutra* (*Lotus of the True Dharma*), one of the most important Mahayana scriptures.
  • 伪蜀 - A term used to distinguish the Later Shu (Shu Kingdom of the Five Dynasties period) from the earlier Western Shu of the Three Kingdoms.
  • 李相炅 - Li Jiong, a high-ranking official of the Later Shu court, likely a Buddhist patron or scholar.
  • 禅宗 - The Zen school of Buddhism, a Chinese adaptation of Indian Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 禅客须知集 - A guide or manual for Zen practitioners.
  • 禅宗祖裔图 - A genealogy of Zen patriarchs, reflecting the lineage of transmission.
  • 阐道歌行偈颂 - Songs, verses, and hymns that explain the Dharma.
  • 禅宗至道集 - A compilation of Zen teachings, possibly the most important work of the master.

  • 释氏法 - The teachings of the Buddha, referring to the Buddhist tradition.
  • 宗门 - The Zen school or the Zen gate, a term referring to the Zen tradition and its teachings.

  • 仙化 - A term used to describe the passing of a sage or spiritual master, often associated with miraculous or transcendent death.
  • 口吐气满屋氛氲 - A description of the spiritual or physical signs of a master's passing, such as a fragrant atmosphere or the release of spiritual energy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居士 - A term for a layperson who follows Buddhist teachings, often translated as 'lay practitioner' or 'lay monk.'
  • 留心禅学 - Means 'to devote oneself to Zen studies,' where '留心' implies a focused or dedicated attention.
  • 三蜀丛林皆尽叅徧 - A complex phrase meaning 'all the Zen monasteries in the Three Shus were thoroughly visited.' '叅徧' (thoroughly visited) is a key idiom in Zen literature.
  • 学流与之切磋 - Means 'scholars and students debated with him,' where '切磋' refers to the exchange of ideas or debate.
  • 话句无逃其确论尔 - A phrase meaning 'his words could not escape his precise judgment,' where '无逃' implies inevitability or inescapability.
  • 示疾 - A polite term for 'falling ill' or 'showing signs of illness.'
  • 行化 - Means 'to go forth and teach' or 'to spread the Dharma.'
  • 透满无形 - A Zen phrase meaning 'to transcend the formless,' referring to enlightenment beyond conceptual boundaries.
  • 十方无碍 - Means 'unobstructed in all directions,' often used to describe the boundless nature of enlightenment.
  • 无心 - Refers to the state of 'no-mind' or 'non-attachment,' a key concept in Zen.
  • 季父为僧 - Means 'his younger uncle is a monk,' where '季父' specifically refers to the younger brother of one's father.
  • 十念 - Refers to the practice of reciting the name of Amitabha Buddha ten times, a common Buddhist ritual.
  • 敬礼瓦屋和尚塔偈 - A poem composed as a tribute to a Buddhist master's stupa (grave).

  • 玄步孤高物外人 - Means 'his steps are profound, solitary, and transcendent,' describing the master's spiritual state.
  • 日本国来寻彼岸 - Means 'coming from Japan to seek the other shore,' where '彼岸' symbolizes enlightenment.
  • 洞山林下过迷津 - Means 'passing through the confused crossing in the mountains and forests,' referring to the journey of spiritual awakening.
  • 流流法乳谁无分 - Means 'the flowing Dharma milk, who can be excluded from it?' referring to the universal availability of the Dharma.
  • 了了教知我最亲 - Means 'clearly teaching that I am closest to it,' emphasizing the master's direct transmission of the Dharma.
  • 一百六十三岁后 - Refers to 'after one hundred sixty-three years,' indicating the master's long life.
  • 方于此塔葬全身 - Means 'only then was his entire body buried in this stupa,' referring to the master's passing and burial.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 四十余年留心禅学 - The phrase is elliptical but means 'for over forty years, he devoted himself to Zen studies.'
  • 三蜀丛林皆尽叅徧 - The phrase is grammatically compact but means 'all the Zen monasteries in the Three Shus were thoroughly visited.'
  • 学流与之切磋，话句无逃其确论尔 - The sentence is a compressed statement meaning 'scholars and students debated with him, and his words could not escape his precise judgment.'
  • 吾期某日行化 - The phrase is elliptical but means 'I will go forth and teach on a certain day.'
  • 透满无形，十方无碍，直至无心，未得为了，何况有念者哉 - The sentence is a series of linked phrases that must be read as a single argument about the nature of enlightenment.
  • 敬礼瓦屋和尚塔偈 - The phrase is a formal introduction to the poem, indicating its purpose as a tribute.
  • 大空无尽刼成尘，玄步孤高物外人 - The poetic couplet is a metaphorical description of the master's spiritual state.
  • 日本国来寻彼岸，洞山林下过迷津 - The couplet describes the master's journey and enlightenment.
  • 流流法乳谁无分，了了教知我最亲 - The couplet emphasizes the universal availability of the Dharma and the master's direct transmission.
  • 一百六十三岁后，方于此塔葬全身 - The sentence is a compressed statement about the master's long life and eventual burial.

  • 洞山悟本禅师 - The name of the Zen master whose teachings are referenced.
  • 碧鸡坊宅 - The name of the location in Chengdu, Sichuan.
  • 伪永泰军节度使禄虔扆 - The name of the historical figure who supported the Zen monastery.

  • 漂至新渠江口 - Means 'it drifted to the mouth of the Xinqu River.'
  • 有识者云 - Means 'those who recognized it said.'
  • 唐山人诗瓢也 - Means 'it was the poetry gourd of Tang Mountain Man.'
  • 探得之，已遭漂润损坏 - Means 'when they retrieved it, it was already damaged by the river.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 病夫顏亦紅 - Literal meaning: 'the sick man's face also turns red.' Likely refers to the effect of alcohol, a common theme in Classical Chinese literature.
  • 贈行如上人云 - Literal meaning: 'giving a gift to a departing person as if speaking to an elder.' Indicates a form of farewell or blessing.
  • 不知名利苦 - Literal meaning: 'do not know the bitterness of fame and profit.' Philosophical statement about detachment from worldly desires.
  • 念佛老岷沲 - Literal meaning: 'chanting Buddha's name until old age in the Min River.' Refers to a life dedicated to Buddhist practice.
  • 衲補云千片 - Literal meaning: 'patching the robe with thousands of pieces.' Metaphor for the accumulation of experiences or wisdom.
  • 香焚篆一窠 - Literal meaning: 'burning incense in a patterned container.' Common practice in Buddhist rituals.
  • 戀山人事少 - Literal meaning: 'loving the mountains means few worldly affairs.' Indicates a life focused on nature and simplicity.
  • 怜客道心多 - Literal meaning: 'caring for guests means many spiritual thoughts.' Suggests a life rich in spiritual reflection.
  • 日日斋钟后 - Literal meaning: 'after the daily bell for fasting.' Refers to the routine of Buddhist practice.
  • 高悬滤水罗 - Literal meaning: 'hanging high a net to filter water.' Metaphor for spiritual purification.
  • 题青城山范贤观云 - Literal meaning: 'writing a poem about the Fan Xian Temple on Qingcheng Mountain.' Reference to a specific location and event.
  • 数里缘山不厌难 - Literal meaning: 'several miles along the mountain, not tiring of the difficulty.' Indicates perseverance in a challenging journey.
  • 为寻真诀问黄冠 - Literal meaning: 'to seek the true secret, asking the Taoist priest.' Refers to the pursuit of spiritual knowledge.
  • 苔铺翠点仙桥滑 - Literal meaning: 'moss covering the green spots on the fairy bridge, making it slippery.' Vivid description of a natural scene.
  • 松织香梢古道寒 - Literal meaning: 'pine trees weaving fragrant branches on the ancient path, making it cold.' Another vivid description of a natural scene.
  • 昼傍绿畦锄嫩玉 - Literal meaning: 'in the daytime, beside the green fields, hoeing tender jade.' Metaphor for tending to crops or spiritual cultivation.
  • 夜开红灶捻新丹 - Literal meaning: 'at night, opening the red stove to knead new elixir.' Reference to alchemical practices.
  • 钟声已断泉声在 - Literal meaning: 'the sound of the bell has stopped, but the sound of the spring remains.' Metaphor for the enduring nature of certain truths.
  • 风动瑶花月满坛 - Literal meaning: 'the wind moves the jade flowers, and the moon fills the altar.' Poetic description of a serene scene.
  • 夫艹泽间有隐逸得志者 - Literal meaning: 'among the grassy wetlands, there are recluses who have achieved their goals.' Refers to hermits who have found contentment.
  • 以经籍自娯 - Literal meaning: 'entertaining themselves with scriptures.' Indicates a life of scholarly pursuit.
  • 诗酒怡情 - Literal meaning: 'poetry and wine to soothe the soul.' Common theme in Classical Chinese literature.
  • 不耀文彩 - Literal meaning: 'not showing off literary talent.' Indicates humility.
  • 不扬姓名 - Literal meaning: 'not publicizing their names.' Indicates a desire for anonymity.
  • 其趋附茍且得无愧赧唐山人乎 - Literal meaning: 'how can they not feel ashamed to follow others in a half-hearted way compared to the people of Tangshan.' Rhetorical question about integrity.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 深得其用 - Phrase meaning 'deeply understood its use', indicating mastery of a practice.
  • 休复尝读道书登真隐诀云 - Phrase meaning 'Xiu Fu once read the *Taoist Book of Ascending to Truth and Seclusion*', introducing a text.
  • 解化之道有八焉 - Phrase meaning 'there are eight ways of transformation', indicating a set of practices.

  • 笑道之辈但见狼藉乞丐于廛市 - Phrase meaning 'those who mock the Dao only see the mess and beggars in the market', criticizing the superficial understanding of others.
  • 以为口实非其所知 - Phrase meaning 'they take it as a mouthful without knowing it', indicating that they misunderstand the true nature of the practices.
  • 然一度托解，须敛迹他方 - Phrase meaning 'however, once one entrusts oneself to transformation, one must hide one's tracks elsewhere', describing the secrecy required in these practices.
  • 屡更名姓，忽逢遇知识露少踪 - Phrase meaning 'often changing names, suddenly meeting someone who knows and revealing a few traces', describing the secretive nature of these practices.
  • 由以激后人 - Phrase meaning 'thereby inspiring later generations', indicating the purpose of these practices.
  • 非奉道好奇者，孰能采摭其隐显尔 - Phrase meaning 'if not for those who follow the Dao and seek the unusual, who could gather and understand its hidden and revealed aspects', emphasizing the rarity of those who truly understand these practices.

  • 財物消盡 - 財物消盡 means 'all their wealth is exhausted.'
  • 車馬屋宇皆為他人所有 - 車馬屋宇皆為他人所有 means 'their carriages, horses, houses, and buildings all become the property of others.'
  • 其貧如初 - 其貧如初 means 'they become poor again as they were at the beginning.'
  • 嗟乎 - 嗟乎 is an interjection expressing sorrow or regret.
  • 不義之物 - 不義之物 means 'unjustly acquired wealth.'
  • 似有神明所掌得之者 - 似有神明所掌得之者 means 'it seems that those who acquire unjust wealth are under the control of divine forces.'
  • 不罹其禍而身獲存者，鮮矣 - 不罹其禍而身獲存者，鮮矣 means 'those who do not suffer the consequences and survive are few.'
  • 此輩天以殃之 - 此輩天以殃之 means 'these people are punished by heaven.'
  • 其是之謂乎 - 其是之謂乎 means 'is this what it means?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 順寇 - Refers to rebels or bandits, often used in the context of internal strife or rebellion.
  • 益部 - Refers to the region of Yizhou, which is modern-day Sichuan province.
  • 天兵 - Refers to the royal or imperial forces, often used to describe the military actions of the central government.
  • 寛大之诏 - Refers to a lenient edict issued by the government, often during times of peace or reconciliation.
  • 脅從徒黨 - Refers to those who were coerced into joining the rebels, often used to describe civilians who were forced to participate in rebellious activities.
  • 放令歸農 - Refers to the practice of releasing prisoners of war or rebels back to their communities to resume farming.
  • 歌酒娯樂 - Refers to a life of leisure and entertainment, often associated with the upper class.
  • 玩好珍異 - Refers to the collection and enjoyment of rare and valuable items, often associated with the wealthy.
  • 喪葬婚聘 - Refers to important social events such as funerals, weddings, and engagements, which were significant in traditional Chinese society.
  • 踰越僭侈 - Refers to exceeding social norms and being extravagant, often associated with moral decay.
  • 災厲疾疫 - Refers to natural disasters, plagues, and epidemics, which were common causes of social and economic disruption in ancient China.
  • 公私事訟 - Refers to public and private disputes, which were common in traditional Chinese legal and social systems.
  • 財物消盡 - Refers to the depletion of wealth, often due to excessive spending or mismanagement.
  • 車馬屋宇皆為他人所有 - Refers to the loss of property and possessions, often due to financial ruin.
  • 其貧如初 - Refers to returning to a state of poverty, often used to describe a cycle of wealth and poverty.
  • 不義之物 - Refers to wealth acquired through unjust means, often associated with moral and ethical concerns.
  • 似有神明所掌得之者 - Refers to the idea that divine forces control the fate of those who acquire unjust wealth, reflecting a belief in karma or divine retribution.
  • 不罹其禍而身獲存者，鮮矣 - Refers to the rarity of those who escape the consequences of their actions, reflecting a belief in the inevitability of divine justice.
  • 此輩天以殃之 - Refers to the idea that these people are punished by heaven, reflecting a belief in divine justice.
  • 其是之謂乎 - Refers to the question of whether this is what it means, reflecting a reflective and philosophical tone.

  • 诸州巡检作坊使 - A title of an official responsible for inspecting and supervising workshops, reflecting the administrative structure of the time.
  • 广武卒 - Refers to a type of soldier, indicating the military context.
  • 怀安军 - A military unit or region, reflecting the administrative and military organization of the Song Dynasty.
  • 金堂古城汉州 - Names of regions, indicating the geographical scope of the events.
  • 张密学 - The name of a high-ranking official, reflecting the political structure of the time.
  • 上官正 - The name of another official, reflecting the political structure of the time.
  • 卭州 - Name of a region, indicating the geographical scope of the events.
  • 嘉眉州 - Name of a region, indicating the geographical scope of the events.
  • 新津江 - The name of a river, indicating the geographical context.
  • 方井 - The name of a well, indicating a specific location.
  • 川界 - Refers to the region, indicating the geographical scope of the events.
  • 为其下广武卒刘旴等谋杀之 - A complex sentence structure where '为其下' indicates that the action was carried out by his subordinates, and '谋杀之' means 'to plot against and kill him.'
  • 韩踰垣而免 - '韩' refers to the official named Han Jingyou, '踰垣' means 'leaped over the wall,' and '免' means 'escaped.'
  • 军贼掠怀安军，及明取金堂古城汉州 - '军贼' refers to military rebels or bandits, and '掠' means 'plundered.' '及明' means 'by dawn,' and '取' means 'captured' or 'took.'
  • 凡六日，行五百余里，劫掠五军州十镇县 - '凡' means 'in total,' and '行' means 'traveled.' '劫掠' means 'raided and looted,' and '五军州十镇县' refers to five military states and ten townships.
  • 所至处皆不及支梧，驱掠军民，势莫可遏 - '所至处' means 'wherever they went,' and '不及支梧' means 'could not resist.' '驱掠' means 'forced and looted,' and '势莫可遏' means 'the momentum could not be stopped.'
  • 州县震慑，户口奔逃 - '震慑' means 'shocked and intimidated,' and '户口奔逃' means 'people fled.'
  • 时知府张密学谓招安使上官正曰 - '时' means 'at that time,' '知府' is the title of a prefectural governor, '张密学' is the name of the official, '谓' means 'told,' and '招安使上官正' is the name of another official.
  • 贼今日卭州，来日必奔嘉眉州 - '贼' means 'rebels,' and '卭州' and '嘉眉州' are names of regions.
  • 贼若有盘泊处，如鱼得渊，卒难除讨 - '盘泊处' means 'a place to settle,' '如鱼得渊' is a metaphor meaning 'like a fish in water,' and '卒难除讨' means 'it would be difficult to eliminate them.'
  • 君必悔之 - '君' means 'you,' and '悔之' means 'regret it.'
  • 今日请即往移兵渡江，逆而击之，夺其胆气，当尽擒之 - '今日' means 'today,' '请即往' means 'please go immediately,' '移兵渡江' means 'move the troops across the river,' '逆而击之' means 'attack them head-on,' '夺其胆气' means 'seize their courage,' and '当尽擒之' means 'we will capture all of them.'
  • 此上策也 - '此' means 'this,' and '上策' means 'the best strategy.'
  • 时不可失 - '时' means 'time,' and '不可失' means 'cannot be missed.'
  • 上官遂点集兵甲前去 - '上官' refers to the official named Shangguan Zheng, '遂' means 'then,' '点集' means 'assembled,' '兵甲' means 'troops and weapons,' and '前去' means 'went forward.'
  • 过新津江，遇贼食于方井 - '过' means 'passed,' '新津江' is the name of a river, '遇' means 'encountered,' '贼' means 'rebels,' '食' means 'eating,' and '方井' is the name of a well.
  • 驰告张密学 - '驰' means 'rushed,' and '告' means 'informed.'
  • 刘既入井，更欲何逃 - '刘' refers to Liu Xū, '既入井' means 'having entered the well,' and '更欲何逃' means 'how could he escape further?'
  • 日中以捷来告，尽杀其党凯旋 - '日中' means 'midday,' '以捷来告' means 'reported the victory,' '尽杀其党' means 'killed all of their followers,' and '凯旋' means 'returned triumphantly.'
  • 且张公料敌先见，皆此类也 - '且' means 'and,' '张公' refers to Zhang Mǐxué, '料敌先见' means 'foreseeing the enemy,' and '皆此类也' means 'all such cases.'
  • 上官能将其兵，是行也易于摧枯 - '上官' refers to Shangguan Zheng, '能将其兵' means 'was able to lead his troops,' '是行也' means 'this action,' and '易于摧枯' means 'was as easy as breaking dry twigs.'
  • 川界由是肃然 - '川界' refers to the region, '由是' means 'therefore,' and '肃然' means 'became orderly.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 民不聊生 - Refers to the dire conditions of the people during times of war or rebellion.
  • 玉石俱焚 - A metaphor from Chinese philosophy, meaning that both the good and the bad will be destroyed.
  • 哀亡友辞 - A traditional form of literary expression in Chinese culture, often used to mourn the death of a friend or loved one.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸平 - The era name of Emperor Zhenzong of Song, referring to the early 11th century.
  • 益郡 - Refers to the region of Sichuan, historically known as Yizhou, which was a significant area during the Song dynasty.
  • 神卫 - A type of military unit in the Song dynasty, often responsible for guarding the capital and key regions.
  • 天军 - Refers to the imperial army, symbolizing the central authority of the Song dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 咸平 - The era name of Emperor Zhenzong of Song.
  • 益郡 - The historical name for the region of Sichuan.
  • 神卫 - A type of military unit in the Song dynasty.
  • 天军 - The imperial army of the Song dynasty.
  • 哀亡友辞 - A traditional form of literary expression for mourning a deceased friend.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夫如是者 - Formal opening phrase meaning 'thus, in such cases' or 'in this manner.'
  • 命非天耶，天非命耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is fate not from heaven? Is heaven not the source of fate?' Philosophical inquiry into the relationship between destiny and divine will.
  • 前進士 - Refers to a candidate who has passed the imperial examinations but has not yet been officially appointed.
  • 哀亡友 - Means 'mourning a deceased friend.'
  • 辭 - Refers to a memorial or elegy written in memory of someone.
  • 傳 - Refers to a biography or account of someone's life.
  • 庶后之人 - Means 'may future people' or 'may those who come after.'
  • 覽之 - Means 'read it' or 'see it.'
  • 得无伤叹叛君残民之事者是哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Will they not be moved to lament and reflect on acts of rebellion against the sovereign and cruelty to the people?' Evokes a moral response from the reader.
  • 哀亡友辭序 - Title of a memorial or elegy, with '序' indicating that it is a preface or introduction.
  • 亡友楊錫 - Refers to the deceased friend named Yang Xi.
  • 字孝隆 - Means 'with the courtesy name Xiao Long,' where '孝隆' is the courtesy name of Yang Xi.
  • 誠至之士也 - Means 'a truly devoted scholar,' emphasizing Yang Xi's dedication and integrity.
  • 昔與趙郡李畋、蜀郡任玠、南陽張逵洎及結文學友 - Lists Yang Xi's literary friends, including Li Tian from Zhaozhou, Ren Jie from Shuzhou, Zhang Kui from Nanyang, and the author himself.
  • 咸治經義於樂安 - Means 'all studied Confucian classics in Le'an,' indicating the place where they studied.
  • 先生悉潛心於六教 - Means 'the teacher (Yang Xi) devoted himself entirely to the Six Teachings,' referring to the six arts of Confucian education: rites, music, archery, charioteering, calligraphy, and mathematics.
  • 然后觀史傳 - Means 'then he studied historical texts,' indicating the next stage of his learning.
  • 遍百家之說 - Means 'he studied the teachings of all schools of thought,' referring to the diverse philosophical traditions of the time.
  • 探奧索微 - Means 'he explored profound and subtle ideas,' indicating his deep engagement with complex concepts.
  • 取其貫於道者 - Means 'he selected those ideas that were aligned with the Way,' referring to the moral and philosophical principles of Confucianism.
  • 既積中而發外 - Means 'after accumulating knowledge internally, he expressed it externally,' indicating the process of internalizing and then externalizing wisdom.
  • 遂下筆楮文 - Means 'he then wrote on paper,' indicating the act of writing.
  • 譔論考賢士節夫之动静 - Means 'he wrote essays examining the actions and words of virtuous scholars and chaste men,' indicating his focus on moral and ethical behavior.
  • 明古人沿習之廢置 - Means 'he clarified the rise and fall of ancient customs,' indicating his interest in historical trends.
  • 紀績義之大小 - Means 'he recorded the great and small deeds of righteousness,' indicating his attention to both significant and minor acts of virtue.
  • 辨適用之邪正 - Means 'he distinguished between correct and incorrect applications,' indicating his critical evaluation of actions.
  • 不虚美，不隱惡 - Means 'he did not exaggerate praise nor hide faults,' indicating his commitment to honesty and objectivity.
  • 庶達乎心志之所冀也 - Means 'so that he could express the aspirations of his heart,' indicating his goal of conveying his inner thoughts and desires.
  • 日執是道以出身入仕 - Means 'he daily adhered to this principle to enter public service,' indicating his dedication to applying his knowledge in practical ways.
  • 俾其抱策書而不愧怍 - Means 'so that he could hold his books without shame,' indicating his confidence in his knowledge and actions.
  • 持言行以符会同 - Means 'he maintained his words and deeds in harmony with others,' indicating his commitment to consistency and integrity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 六教 - Refers to the six arts of Confucian education: rites, music, archery, charioteering, calligraphy, and mathematics.
  • 百家之說 - Refers to the diverse schools of thought during the Warring States period, including Confucianism, Daoism, Legalism, and others.
  • 經義 - Refers to the study of Confucian classics and their interpretations.
  • 史傳 - Refers to historical texts and biographies.

[83]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未始一日而忘此也 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'never forgot for a single day,' emphasizing persistent memory and emotional attachment.
  • 表其懿行 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'record his virtuous deeds,' emphasizing the importance of preserving the friend's legacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乡贤里能 - Refers to local scholars and capable people, indicating the social and intellectual context of the friend's aspirations.
  • 春官氏 - Refers to the official responsible for overseeing rituals and ceremonies, suggesting the friend's interest in official and ceremonial roles.

[84]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 予取来之得朋兮 - Poetic expression meaning 'I have gained friends by my actions.'
  • 接羣居之及义 - Means 'I have joined the community and adhered to righteousness.'
  • 不殒获于贫贱兮 - Means 'I have not been ruined by poverty and lowly status.'
  • 耻喧呶于声利 - Means 'I am ashamed to seek fame and profit.'
  • 炳旧史之逺目兮 - Means 'I shine like the distant vision of ancient historians.'
  • 饫六经之正味 - Means 'I have savored the true essence of the Six Classics.'
  • 议班纪之九流兮 - Means 'I have discussed the nine schools of thought in the *Bianji* (Classified Catalogue).'
  • 广刘书之七志 - Means 'I have expanded on Liu Xiang's *Qi Zhi* (Seven Categories).'
  • 旣积中之发外兮 - Means 'Having accumulated knowledge within, I now express it outwardly.'
  • 幸入官以莅事 - Means 'I am fortunate to enter office and handle affairs.'
  • 将结绶之弹冠兮 - Means 'I am about to tie the sash and clean the cap,' a metaphor for preparing for official duties.
  • 匪君翔之于坠 - Means 'Not for you to soar and fall,' implying that the friend should not be abandoned in misfortune.
  • 何贼卒之妖兴兮 - Means 'Why do bandits and rebels suddenly rise?'
  • 据藩服之城垒 - Means 'They occupy the fortresses of the frontier regions.'
  • 君岂适逺之无所兮 - Means 'Are you merely far away without purpose?'
  • 奈病来之难起 - Means 'How can you rise from illness?'
  • 我徂西山兮不与子别 - Means 'I go to the West Mountain without bidding you farewell.'
  • 或出处兮其心曷异 - Means 'Whether in office or out, our hearts are the same.'
  • 扰攘之长然 - Means 'The turmoil continues.'
  • 诚会合之密迩 - Means 'Truly, our meetings are close and intimate.'
  • 洎王师之讨平兮 - Means 'When the imperial army has pacified the rebels.'
  • 闻吾友之已矣 - Means 'I hear that my friend has passed away.'
  • 燕雀啁啾兮辽鹤幽 - Means 'The sparrows chirp, while the crane remains silent.'
  • 病豺狼噬囓兮驺虞愤死 - Means 'The sick wolves gnaw, and the righteous officer dies in anger.'
  • 嗟庆绪之不续兮 - Means 'Alas, the lineage of the Qian family is not continued.'
  • 复嗣子之随踬 - Means 'The heir is also hindered.'
  • 徒呼天之云亡 - Means 'Only to cry out to heaven about his death.'
  • 故使其秀疾而神驶 - Means 'Thus, he was brilliant and swift in spirit.'
  • 志愿表其文行兮 - Means 'His aspirations are shown in his writings and deeds.'
  • 示广场之豪士 - Means 'To demonstrate to the learned in the marketplace.'
  • 异知子之若然兮 - Means 'To know you as such.'
  • 今空抑哀以摛辞兮 - Means 'Now, I suppress my sorrow to write these words.'
  • 报亡友之终始 - Means 'To report on my deceased friend's life and death.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 六经 - Refers to the Six Classics: the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), *Shujing* (Book of Documents), *Yijing* (Book of Changes), *Liji* (Book of Rites), *Chunqiu* (Spring and Autumn Annals), and *Lunyu* (Analects of Confucius).
  • 班纪之九流 - Refers to the *Bianji* (Classified Catalogue) and the nine schools of thought.
  • 刘书之七志 - Refers to Liu Xiang's *Qi Zhi* (Seven Categories), a classification of books.
  • 结绶之弹冠 - A metaphor for preparing for official duties, derived from the custom of tying the sash and cleaning the cap before taking office.
  • 贼卒 - Refers to bandits and rebels.
  • 藩服 - Refers to the frontier regions of the empire.
  • 庆绪 - Refers to the lineage of the Qian family, a noble family.
  • 嗣子 - Refers to the heir.
  • 驺虞 - A mythical creature often associated with righteousness and justice.

Proper Nouns

  • 六经 - The Six Classics.
  • 班纪 - The *Bianji* (Classified Catalogue).
  • 刘书 - Liu Xiang's *Qi Zhi* (Seven Categories).
  • 驺虞 - A mythical creature.
  • 庆绪 - The lineage of the Qian family.
  • 嗣子 - The heir.

[85]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 攻詩 - Literally 'to study poetry,' but in classical usage implies a deep and dedicated study of the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), a core text in the Confucian canon.
  • 性柔而惰 - 'Soft in nature and lazy,' a characterization of someone who is gentle but lacks ambition or drive.
  • 或謂其性懦，非能立事 - 'Some said his nature was timid, not capable of accomplishing anything.' This is a common classical structure where a character's personality is judged by others.
  • 未竟 - 'Not completed' or 'not achieved,' referring to郝逢's failed attempt to obtain a recommendation.
  • 屬盜起於境 - 'When bandits arose in the region,' a classical expression indicating the outbreak of disorder.
  • 資產略盡 - 'His property was almost entirely lost,' indicating total economic ruin.
  • 迫寒餒而無憂嘆 - 'Beset by cold and hunger, yet without lamentation or complaint.' This is a classical way to highlight a person's endurance and stoicism.
  • 失律 - 'To violate the law or military discipline,' often used in the context of military failure.
  • 兵亂為賊盜 - 'The soldiers turned into bandits,' indicating a breakdown in military order.
  • 殺守臣而據郡 - 'They killed the local governor and seized the city,' a common pattern in classical narratives of rebellion.
  • 自春徂秋 - 'From spring to autumn,' a classical time expression indicating a long period of conflict.
  • 驅老幼以守城 - 'They drove the elderly and children to guard the city,' a grim description of the desperate measures taken during a siege.
  • 或獻謀於賊，令盡索郡中書生署職俾立効 - 'Some offered strategies to the bandits, ordering them to search for all the scholars in the region and appoint them to positions to demonstrate their effectiveness.' This is a complex sentence structure, typical of classical Chinese.
  • 凡得數十輩 - 'They gathered dozens of such individuals,' referring to the scholars forced into service.
  • 列兵而脅曰 - 'They lined them up with soldiers and threatened them, saying…'
  • 不從者即此誅戮，仍及其族 - 'Those who do not comply will be executed here, and their families will suffer as well.' A standard classical warning.
  • 皆震懾而從 - 'All were terrified and complied,' showing the effectiveness of the threat.
  • 逢前绐賊帥曰 - 'Hao Feng stepped forward and deceived the bandit leader, saying…' The verb '绐' means to deceive or mislead.
  • 念所索儒士，某非儒，豈可徼祿 - 'Considering the scholars you are seeking, I am not a scholar, how could I seek your salary?' This is a clever and defiant response.
  • 詞氣剛憤，不可屈抗 - 'His words and tone were resolute and angry, unyielding and unbreakable.' A classical way of describing a person's firmness.
  • 賊怒令引去 - 'The bandit leader was angry and ordered him to be taken away.'
  • 臨刃復召者三 - 'Three times, as he was about to be executed, he was called back.' This is a dramatic and emotionally charged sentence.
  • 會解於賊，槚楚而釋之 - 'By chance he was released from the bandits, though he was beaten with a stick (槚楚).'
  • 既獲免，遂匿於家 - 'After being spared, he hid in his home.'
  • 或聞於郡守將，上其事而中止 - 'Some reported this to the local governor, who submitted the matter to the authorities and it was halted.' This suggests that郝逢's actions were brought to the attention of the authorities but the matter was not pursued.
  • 逢亦不復言 - 'Hao Feng also did not speak of it again,' indicating he chose silence or discretion.
  • 居貧自若 - 'He lived in poverty as usual,' suggesting that he was not affected by the hardships, maintaining his composure and dignity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 郝逢字致尧 - Hao Feng, whose courtesy name is Zhi Yao. In classical Chinese, the surname is given first, followed by the courtesy name.
  • 成都人 - A city in Sichuan province, historically an important cultural and economic center.
  • 鄉薦 - A system in the imperial examination system where local officials could recommend talented individuals for higher examination.
  • 盜起於境 - Refers to the outbreak of banditry or rebellion within the region.
  • 蜀掌兵者失律 - A reference to a military commander in the region of Shu (Sichuan) who violated military discipline, leading to chaos.
  • 守臣 - The local governor or official responsible for governing a region.
  • 書生 - Literally 'book scholars,' referring to educated individuals, often candidates for the imperial exams.
  • 署職 - To appoint to an official position, often temporarily or as a favor.
  • 賊帥 - The leader of a bandit or rebel group.
  • 天兵 - A classical term for the imperial army or heavenly troops, often used to refer to the legitimate military forces of the state.
  • 逆黨 - Rebels or traitors, those opposing the legitimate authority.
  • 郡守將 - The local governor or military commander.
  • 上其事而中止 - To submit the matter to the authorities and halt further action, suggesting that郝逢's actions were reported but not pursued.

Proper Nouns

  • 郝逢 - Hao Feng, the subject of the biography.
  • 致尧 - Zhi Yao, his courtesy name.
  • 成都 - Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan province.
  • 蜀 - Shu, the ancient name for the region of Sichuan.
  • 天兵 - A classical term for the imperial army, often used metaphorically.
  • 郡守将 - The local governor or military commander.

[86]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 噫 - Interjection expressing surprise, wonder, or emotion, often translated as 'Ah!' or 'Alas!'
  • 當是時 - Refers to a specific time or period, often translated as 'At that time' or 'During that period.'
  • 有位者 - Refers to people in positions of power or authority, often translated as 'those in power' or 'the powerful.'
  • 茍命 - Means 'to live by chance' or 'to survive by luck,' often translated as 'to live by luck' or 'to survive by chance.'
  • 致命 - Literally 'to give one's life,' but here it means 'to sacrifice oneself' or 'to risk one's life.'
  • 陷非義 - Means 'to fall into injustice' or 'to commit an unjust act.'
  • 同禄衒勢 - Refers to people who flaunt their wealth and power, often translated as 'those who flaunt their wealth and power.'
  • 晦迹 - Means 'to conceal one's identity' or 'to hide one's presence.'
  • 混于俗 - Means 'to blend in with the crowd' or 'to live a mundane life.'
  • 人不甚知 - Means 'not well-known by others' or 'little known by people.'

  • 园中有慈竹藂生，根不离母，故名之慈也 - Describes the bamboo: 'In the garden, there are bamboo plants that grow in clusters, with roots not far from the mother plant, hence named '慈' (filial).'
  • 有钓丝竹，以其弱杪低而垂至地也 - Describes another type of bamboo: 'There is fishing pole bamboo, which is weak and droops to the ground at the tip.'
  • 有丝竹，叶细而青茎，瘦而紫，亦谓之墨竹 - Describes yet another type of bamboo: 'There is silk bamboo, with fine, green leaves and thin, purple stems, also called '墨竹' (ink bamboo).'
  • 有对青竹，身黄色，有一脉青节节相对，故谓之对青也 - Describes yet another type of bamboo: 'There is paired green bamboo, with a yellow body and green nodes that are paired, hence named '对青' (paired green).'
  • 有苦竹，叶秾多阴，笋高之时粉香箨翠 - Describes yet another type of bamboo: 'There is bitter bamboo, with dense, shady leaves and fragrant, green shoots when they are high.'
  • 有柱竹，扶疎藂茂，潇洒亭台，无出此数君也 - Describes yet another type of bamboo: 'There is pillar bamboo, lush and graceful, fitting well in pavilions and terraces, none of these types of bamboo are out of place.'
  • 俗以五月十三日种竹多遭烈日晒干，园中竹以八月社前后 - Describes the timing of planting bamboo: 'Commonly, bamboo planted on May 13th often dries up due to intense sunlight, but in the garden, bamboo is planted before and after the August social day.'
  • 是月天色多阴土润，竹以此月行根也 - Explains why bamboo is planted in August: 'This month, the sky is often overcast and the soil is moist, which is the best time for bamboo to establish roots.'
  • 凡欲移竹，先掘地坑，令寛大，以水调细土作稀泥，即掘竹，四面凿断大根，科连根以绳锢定，舁时勿令动着根须 - Describes the detailed steps of transplanting bamboo: 'Whenever you want to move bamboo, first dig a wide pit and mix water with fine soil to make a thin mud. Then dig up the bamboo, cut off the large roots on all sides, bind the roots together with rope, and lift it carefully without disturbing the root hairs.'

  • 然后寻美果枝 - A sequence marker meaning 'then seek beautiful fruit branches.'
  • 选来年有鹤膝向阳者 - A conditional phrase meaning 'select those that will have crane-knee and face the sun next year.'

  • 欲不泄其气也 - A purpose clause meaning 'in order to prevent the loss of their vitality.'
  • 冬至后十日立春前七日 - A specific time frame meaning 'ten days after the winter solstice and seven days before the beginning of spring.'

  • 取美枝或一枝或两枝 - A sequence marker meaning 'take a beautiful branch, one or two.'
  • 斜捭，勿伤其皮 - A sequence marker meaning 'insert them diagonally, without damaging the bark.'
  • 插于野树罅中 - A sequence marker meaning 'insert them into the cleft of the wild tree.'

  • 等紧密用牛粪泥封之 - A sequence marker meaning 'seal it tightly with cow dung mud.'

  • 以麻纫缠定 - A sequence marker meaning 'tie it securely with hemp thread.'
  • 上更以黄土泥塔头裹之 - A sequence marker meaning 'cover the top with yellow earth mud to form a mound.'
  • 勿使雨水透入 - A purpose clause meaning 'to prevent rainwater from penetrating.'
  • 或有野树旁生芽叶，即取去之 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if any wild tree sprouts buds or leaves on the side, remove them immediately.'
  • 若依此法，则当年有花必矣 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if you follow this method, flowers will definitely bloom in the same year.'
  • 休复尝依其教而树，树皆成 - A sequence marker meaning 'I once followed this advice and planted trees, and all of them grew successfully.'
  • 不喻其野树子实酸涩，鹤膝枝甜美 - A descriptive clause meaning 'not understanding that the wild tree seeds are sour and astringent, while the crane-knee branches are sweet.'
  • 接酸涩树上，为酸涩之气所推 - A causal clause meaning 'grafting onto a sour and astringent tree, it is pushed by the sour and astringent energy.'
  • 又焉得遂于甜美耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how could it then become sweet?'
  • 树之元气反不能推小枝而与之俱酸涩 - A causal clause meaning 'the tree's vital energy cannot push the small branches to become sour and astringent along with it.'
  • 何也 - A rhetorical question meaning 'why is that?'
  • 所谓本不胜末，而物性难解欤 - A causal clause meaning 'it is said that the root cannot overcome the branch, and the nature of things is hard to understand.'
  • 今之人但荫其枝叶，食其美实，而不求其酸涩所推耶 - A conditional phrase meaning 'today's people merely enjoy the shade of the branches and eat the delicious fruit, without seeking the sour and astringent qualities that are pushed by the tree.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 野树 - Refers to wild trees, often used in agricultural and horticultural contexts.
  • 鹤膝 - A term used to describe a specific type of branch shape, resembling the knee of a crane, often associated with desirable fruit tree branches.
  • 牛粪泥 - Cow dung mud, used in traditional Chinese horticulture for its fertilizing properties.
  • 笋箨 - Bamboo shoots and leaves, used for wrapping and protecting grafts.
  • 麻纫 - Hemp thread, used for tying and securing grafts.
  • 黄土泥塔头 - Yellow earth mud used to form a mound over the graft, providing protection and moisture retention.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '凡欲接果，先得野树子酸涩不美者，如臂已上皆堪接也' must be read as a series of steps for grafting, starting with selecting the right wild tree seeds.
  • The sequence '然后寻美果枝，选来年有鹤膝向阳者，枝长不过二尺，过则难治' outlines the selection criteria for the fruit branches to be grafted.
  • The sequence '至时剪下，便札于萝卜中，欲不泄其气也' describes the immediate steps after cutting the branches, emphasizing the importance of preserving their vitality.
  • The time frame '冬至后十日立春前七日，其野树皮润萌芽未发' is crucial for the success of the grafting process.
  • The sequence '将野树以锯截人去地五七寸，中心劈破深二寸许' describes the precise cutting and splitting of the wild tree.
  • The sequence '取美枝或一枝或两枝，斜捭，勿伤其皮，插于野树罅中' details the insertion of the selected branches into the wild tree.
  • The sequence '外与野树皮相齐，等紧密用牛粪泥封之，与笋箨包裹其接处，以麻纫缠定' outlines the sealing and wrapping of the graft site.
  • The sequence '上更以黄土泥塔头裹之，勿使雨水透入' describes the final steps to protect the graft from the elements.
  • The conditional phrase '或有野树旁生芽叶，即取去之' emphasizes the importance of removing any unwanted growth.
  • The conditional phrase '若依此法，则当年有花必矣' predicts the success of the grafting method.
  • The sequence marker '休复尝依其教而树，树皆成' provides a personal anecdote about the success of the method.
  • The descriptive clause '不喻其野树子实酸涩，鹤膝枝甜美' explains the contrast between the wild tree seeds and the desired fruit branches.
  • The causal clause '接酸涩树上，为酸涩之气所推，又焉得遂于甜美耶' explains why the grafting method may not work as intended.
  • The causal clause '树之元气反不能推小枝而与之俱酸涩' further explains the limitations of the method.
  • The rhetorical question and causal clause '何也，所谓本不胜末，而物性难解欤' reflect on the complexity of the natural world.
  • The conditional phrase '今之人但荫其枝叶，食其美实，而不求其酸涩所推耶' critiques modern people for focusing only on the immediate benefits of the tree.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼得而诛之；为恶于显者，人得而诛之乎 - A rhetorical question comparing the punishment of evil deeds by ghosts in the underworld to punishment by humans in the world. It reflects a belief in divine retribution.
  • 明神祷之而求福，有之乎 - A question about whether praying to divine spirits can bring blessings.
  • 今冥中所重者罪，在是何等 - A question asking what sins are most significant in the underworld.
  • 应者曰：杀生与负心尔 - The response identifies killing and betraying trust as major sins.
  • 所景奉者浮图教也 - A statement indicating that the underworld follows Buddhist teachings.
  • 某之后事可得闻乎 - A question asking about future matters.
  • 祸福之事不可前告 - A statement that future fortunes and misfortunes cannot be predicted.
  • 神道幽秘，弗许预知也 - Another statement emphasizing the mysterious nature of the divine realm, which cannot be known in advance.
  • 今欲酬君，君欲希我何物 - A question asking what the spirit wants in return for its favor.
  • 应者曰：望君济我资镪数百千贯 - The response requests a large sum of money.
  • 知微辞之，应者曰：所求者非世间铜铁为者，乃楮货尔 - The response clarifies that the requested money is not physical currency but paper money.
  • 知微乃许之 - A statement that the person agrees to the request.
  • 应者曰：烧时慎勿使着地，可以薪艹荐籍之，向一处以火爇，不得搅剔，其钱则不破碎，一一可达也 - Instructions on how to burn the paper money properly.
  • 遂依教蟠纸钱数百千贯 - A statement that the person follows the instructions and burns the paper money.
  • 噫，昔汉世以前，未知幽冥以何为赂遗之物尔 - A concluding remark expressing that before the Han dynasty, it was unclear what was used to bribe or offer to the underworld.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浮图教 - Refers to Buddhism, which was introduced to China during the Han dynasty.
  • 楮货 - Refers to paper money, which became common in China during the Song dynasty.
  • 汉世以前 - Refers to the period before the Han dynasty, emphasizing the historical context of the passage.

Proper Nouns

  • 浮图教 - Buddhism.
  • 楮货 - Paper money.
  • 汉世 - The Han dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 葢取諸國風雅頌之詩聲其章句，以律和之之謂也 - Explains that these songs are derived from the poetic forms of the *Guofeng*, *Ya*, and *Song* sections of the *Shijing* (Book of Songs), and are harmonized according to musical scales.
  • 非歌詩之言，則無以成其調也 - Emphasizes that without the lyrics of the songs, the melodies could not be formed.
