  • 因兄能画，相学习而顿悟 - '因兄能画' means 'because his brother could paint,' and '相学习而顿悟' means 'they learned from each other and suddenly understood.' The phrase '顿悟' (sudden enlightenment) is key to understanding the rapid mastery.
  • 若生而知之 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'as if he knew it from birth,' emphasizing innate talent or quick learning.
  • 大凡性有巧拙，画无古今 - '大凡' means 'generally,' '性有巧拙' refers to 'natural talent or clumsiness,' and '画无古今' means 'painting has no distinction between ancient and modern,' suggesting that skill transcends time.
  • 俾童君画鲍倩五禽图 - '俾' means 'to have someone do something,' and '鲍倩五禽图' refers to a specific painting of the 'Five Animals' attributed to Bao Qian.
  • 优礼待之 - Means 'treated him with courtesy and respect.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蜀末归命，圣宋以前 - Refers to the late Shu dynasty (a state during the Three Kingdoms period) and the early Song dynasty, indicating a historical timeframe.
  • 张杜二人 - Refers to Zhang and Du, two renowned painters of Buddhist figures and arhats.
  • 张南本 - A painter known for his depictions of people, carriages, and horses.
  • 黄伯鸾 - A painter known for his work on birds, sparrows, bamboo, and stones.
  • 李升 - A painter known for his landscapes.
  • 李文秀 - A painter known for his portraits.
  • 童君与前辈不相下也 - Suggests that the young scholar is on par with his predecessors in skill.
  • 鲍倩五禽图 - Refers to a famous painting of the 'Five Animals' (a set of exercises or symbols) attributed to Bao Qian, a historical figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 童处士 - The title of the young scholar.
  • 张杜二人 - Zhang and Du, two historical painters.
  • 张南本 - A historical painter.
  • 黄伯鸾 - A historical painter.
  • 李升 - A historical painter.
  • 李文秀 - A historical painter.
  • 鲍倩 - A historical figure associated with the 'Five Animals' painting.
  • 海云山寺 - A specific temple.
  • 大圣慈寺 - A specific temple.
  • 玉局化龙虎君 - A specific temple or site.
  • 天庆观 - A specific temple or site.
  • 圣祖殿 - A specific hall or shrine.

[130]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 祥符中 - Reign period of Emperor Zhenzong of Song (998–1022); denotes a specific era.
  • 愚茅亭 - Personal or humble reference to a scholar or artist; possibly the author or a close associate.
  • 时辈皆云 - Common expression meaning 'contemporaries all say'; indicates a widespread opinion.
  • 弹琴非是乐 - Phrase suggesting that playing the qin (a traditional Chinese zither) is not considered a form of entertainment.
  • 写真非是画 - Phrase suggesting that portrait painting is not considered a form of art.
  • 春秋左氏传 - Refers to the Zuo Zhuan, an ancient Chinese commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals.
  • 晋侯观于军府 - Reference to a scene in the Zuo Zhuan where Duke Wen of Jin visits the military treasury.
  • 南冠而絷者谁也 - Phrase from the Zuo Zhuan meaning 'Who is the person with a southern cap and fettered?'
  • 有司对曰 - Formal expression meaning 'the official replied.'
  • 郑人所献楚囚也 - Phrase from the Zuo Zhuan meaning 'It is a Chu captive presented by the people of Zheng.'
  • 使税之召而吊之 - Formal expression meaning 'the duke ordered him to be released and summoned to pay respects.'
  • 再拜稽首 - Formal expression meaning 'to bow twice and prostrate oneself'; a sign of deep respect.
  • 先父之职官也 - Phrase meaning 'the duty of my father'; indicates a hereditary position.
  • 敢有二事 - Phrase meaning 'I dare not have two tasks'; indicates a commitment to a single duty.
  • 操南风 - Phrase meaning 'to play the Southern Wind'; a piece of music.
  • 杜预注云 - Reference to Du Yu's commentary on the Zuo Zhuan.
  • 伶人乐官也 - Phrase meaning 'a musician and official of music.'
  • 南齐谢赫论画有六法 - Reference to Xie He's Six Principles of Painting from the Southern Qi dynasty.
  • 气韵生动 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'vital spirit and movement.'
  • 骨法用笔 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'structure and use of brushwork.'
  • 应物像形 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'responding to objects and their forms.'
  • 随类传彩 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'following categories and transmitting colors.'
  • 经营位置 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'planning and placement.'
  • 传移模写 - One of the Six Principles, meaning 'transmission and imitation.'
  • 写真者 - Term meaning 'portrait painter.'
  • 于画六法中一法尔 - Phrase meaning 'one of the six principles of painting.'
  • 膳夫和羮 - Metaphor for the harmonious blending of flavors; often used to describe the balance in art or governance.
  • 酰醢盐梅以烹鱼肉 - Phrase meaning 'vinegar, soy sauce, salt, and plum to cook fish and meat'; refers to the ingredients needed for a balanced dish.
  • 齐之以味 - Phrase meaning 'to harmonize with taste.'
  • 阙一不可 - Phrase meaning 'one cannot be missing'; emphasizes the importance of each component.
  • 国朝取士于诗赋策论 - Phrase meaning 'the current dynasty selects officials based on poetry, essays, and policy discussions.'
  • 君子之道贵乎全也 - Phrase meaning 'the way of the gentleman values completeness.'
  • 画与学，虽殊功用奚异 - Phrase meaning 'although painting and learning differ in function, they are not different in their essence.'
  • 君其全和 - Phrase meaning 'you should strive for harmony and completeness.'
  • 益虽不敏，请事斯语矣 - Phrase meaning 'I may not be very intelligent, but I will follow this advice.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 祥符中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of Song, a period known for its cultural and artistic achievements.
  • 愚茅亭 - Likely a reference to a scholar or artist, possibly the author or a close associate.
  • 春秋左氏传 - An ancient Chinese commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals, a significant historical text.
  • 晋侯观于军府 - A scene from the Zuo Zhuan, a classic text that provides commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals.
  • 南齐谢赫论画有六法 - Refers to Xie He's Six Principles of Painting, a foundational theory in Chinese art.
  • 膳夫和羮 - A metaphor for the harmonious blending of flavors, often used to describe the balance in art or governance.
  • 国朝取士于诗赋策论 - Refers to the selection process for officials in the Song dynasty, which valued literary and intellectual abilities.

Proper Nouns

  • 祥符中 - Reign period of Emperor Zhenzong of Song.
  • 愚茅亭 - A personal or humble reference to a scholar or artist.
  • 春秋左氏传 - The Zuo Zhuan, an ancient Chinese commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals.
  • 晋侯 - Duke Wen of Jin, a historical figure mentioned in the Zuo Zhuan.
  • 南齐 - The Southern Qi dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
  • 谢赫 - Xie He, a Chinese art critic and theorist.
  • 国朝 - The current dynasty, in this case, the Song dynasty.
